cot_prefix: Okay, let's find out the answer to the question. The question is asking to fill in the blank in the sentence "Smith became very rich, because he ___ gave anything to anybody." The options are (A) always, (B) never, (C) often, (D) sometimes. First, I need to refer back to the story provided. The first sentence of the story says: "Smith was a landlord. He loved money very much and never gave anything to anybody. Soon he became rich." So from the story, we know that Smith loved money and never gave anything to anyone. As a result of this behavior, he became rich. The blank in the question is asking for the reason why he became rich. The logical answer would be the one that matches the information given in the story. Looking at the options: - (A) always: If he always gave things, he probably wouldn't become rich. - (B) never: This matches the story, as it says he never gave anything. - (C) often: If he often gave, that's the opposite of becoming rich through not sharing. - (D) sometimes: This is not the right fit either because
